stories.title: Lotto 6aus49 am Mittwoch: Das sind die Gewinnzahlen vom 22.7.2026
stories.summary: A 50-million-euro jackpot in the German Lotto 6aus49 was up for grabs on July 22, 2026, with a 1 in 140 million chance of winning. The winning numbers were 3, 5, 10, 14, 25, and 49, with a superzahl of 3. The jackpot remains unclaimed, and players can still check their tickets for a possible win.

stories.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
stories.summary: A woman and her family were left "terrified" after discovering a landlord hiding behind a wall in a Co Wexford holiday home they had booked through Booking.com. The incident occurred last weekend when Therese and her family returned from a family party to find the landlord behind the wall. Booking.com has launched an investigation and suspended the property while the investigation is completed. The family's booking was for the entire property, which was advertised as owner-free.

stories.title: World Matchplay Darts: Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Gerwyn Price, James Wade, Gian van Veen in action
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies has been eliminated from the 2026 World Matchplay Darts after a stunning upset on Monday night, knocking out Luke Humphries with a memorable 170 checkout. Menzies now faces Ross Smith, who has won two titles this season and opened the tournament with a 10-6 victory over Kevin Doets. Smith averaged 100.55 and fired in seven 180s in his first match. The two players will face off in the next round.

stories.title: « Je suis de retour » : pour la quatrième fois, Kylian Mbappé va figurer sur la pochette d'EA Sports FC
stories.summary: Kylian Mbappé returns as the cover athlete for EA Sports FC 27, marking his fourth appearance on the franchise's cover, equaling Lionel Messi's record. The Real Madrid striker, who last featured on the cover four years ago while at Paris Saint-Germain, will appear on the game set for release on September 25, 2026. His selection follows his standout performance as the all-time top scorer in World Cup history. The announcement comes after EA Sports revealed Mbappé will headline the Ultimate Edition, succeeding Jude Bellingham and Jamal Musiala on the standard cover.

stories.title: Le PSG a fait une grosse offre à Ferran Torres
stories.summary: Paris Saint-Germain has made a lucrative offer to Spanish striker Ferran Torres following his decisive winning goal in the 2026 World Cup final. The twenty-six-year-old forward is currently at odds with FC Barcelona over contract terms and playing time under new manager Hansi Flick. While the Catalan club intends to retain him, Torres has reportedly rejected their latest proposal. This summer transfer window could see the international star move to Paris.

stories.title: IFC: First Look at The Stunt Driver
stories.summary: We need to write a punchy 3-4 sentence brief, 60-90 words, for a short news video. Lead with most important fact. Include key who/what/where and number, date or consequence that matters. Add one line of context or what happens next. Prefer facts that agree across outlets; ignore contradictions. No repeated words or sentences. No markdown, hashtags, brackets, quotes, emojis. No mention of "the article" or outlet names. Plain text only. We need to extract agreed facts: - Film: The Stunt Driver - Starring: Jay Baruchel as Ken Carter (Canadian daredevil) - Also starring: Laurence Leboeuf, Dan Bakkedahl, Ben Foster, Ed Helms - Written and directed by Michael Dowse - Set in mid-seventies, true underdog story of Ken Carter, world champion stunt driver, attempts mile-long jump across St. Lawrence river in a rocket car. - Release: U.S. theaters on September 25 (year? The blu-ray.com says 2026 film set to open September 25. Deadline says Independent Film Company releases the film in U.S. theaters on September 25. No year given but likely 2026? The blu-ray.com says 2026 film. Deadline says acquired ahead of world premiere at Toronto Film Festival (likely 2025? but we can't assume). The agreed fact: release date September 25 (year unspecified but we can include 2026 if agreed? blu-ray.com says 2026, deadline doesn't mention year but says release September 25.

stories.title: Las Feministas del PSOE y 96 asociaciones de España y Latinoamérica cargan contra Bibiana Aído ante ONU por atacar a la relatora de Mujer
stories.summary: The user wants a 3-4 sentence brief (60-90 words) for a short news video based on three Spanish news sources reporting the same event. I need to cross-check facts that agree across outlets. Key facts from all three sources: - Bibiana Aído (former Spanish Equality Minister under Zapatero, now UN Women Regional Director for Latin America/Caribbean based in Panama) - 96+ feminist associations (including FEMAS, Spanish Socialist Feminists Association led by Amelia Valcárcel) from Spain and Latin America (Brazil, Mexico, Colombia) - They sent a letter to UN Secretary General António Guterres - Accusing Aído of attacking/difaming Reem Alsalem (UN Special Rapporteur on Violence Against Women) - Aído distributed a confidential report (from UN Women Brazil office) on June 10 to discredit Alsalem - The report criticized Alsalem's visits to Mexico, Brazil, Colombia - Associations accuse Aído of promoting "queer/trans" ideology, normalizing prostitution as "sex work" and surrogacy as a right All three agree on the core: feminist groups complain to Guterres about Aído targeting Alsalem. Need to lead with most important fact: 96+ feminist associations filed complaint with UN chief against Aído for attacking the UN women's rights rapporteur. Word count target: 60-90 words. 3-4 sentences. Draft: More than 90 feminist organizations from Spain and Latin America have formally complained to UN Secretary-General António Guterres against Bibiana Aído, the UN Women regional director for Latin America.

stories.title: Princess Charlene Glitters in a Gold Elie Saab Gown at Monaco’s Red Cross Ball
stories.summary: We need to produce a 3-4 sentence brief, 60-90 words, punchy, informative, lead with most important fact, include key who/what/where and number, date or consequence that matters; add one line of context or what happens next. Prefer facts that agree across outlets; ignore contradictions. Neutral, factual, no hype, no opinion, no fabrication, no repeated words or sentences. Plain text only — no markdown, hashtags, brackets, quotes, emoji. Do not mention "the article" or outlet names. We have two sources: townandcountrymag.com and hola.com. They both mention Princess Charlene and Prince Albert headlined Monaco’s Red Cross Ball on Saturday, one of the marquee events on the principality’s summer social calendar. They both mention the event date? townandcountrymag.com says "Saturday" but not specific date; hola.com says "July 18" (the 77th Monaco Red Cross Gala). So the date is July 18 (Saturday). Both mention the location: Salle des Étoiles in Monte Carlo (townandcountrymag.com) and the gala (hola.com). They both mention Princess Charlene wearing Elie Saab gown; hola.com mentions soft metallic and ivory tones, coordinated look. They both mention Camille Gottlieb, niece, wearing Elisabetta Franchi gown (townandcountrymag.com) and also mentions her previous homage to Grace Kelly. So we can include that Camille Gottlieb attended, wore a soft yellow Elisabetta Franchi gown. Both mention Prince Albert and Princess Charlene as president and vice president of Monaco Red Cross.

stories.title: Obama Takes Shot at Vance Over Marriage ‘Hypocrisy’
stories.summary: Former President Barack Obama criticized Vice President JD Vance for his stance on immigration, pointing out the hypocrisy of Vance's hardline rhetoric given his own marriage to an immigrant. Vance's wife, Usha, received US citizenship at birth after her parents immigrated from India. Obama suggested Vance's views echo "blood-and-soil" ideas about citizenship. The criticism comes as Vance promotes MAGA ideas on immigration.

stories.title: Casemiro: Inter Miami being investigated for tampering after signing ex-Manchester United midfielder
stories.summary: We need to produce a punchy, informative 3-4 sentence brief (60-90 words). Lead with most important fact: MLS investigating Inter Miami for tampering after signing Casemiro. Include who/what/where and number/date/consequence that matters: Casemiro signed, 34-year-old, contract to end of 2027 MLS season with option to 2029, investigation about discovery rights, LA Galaxy held rights, settlement reached. Add one line of context or what happens next: MLS gathering info, will refrain comment until review complete; terms to be released after investigation. We must avoid repeated words or sentences. No markdown, hashtags, brackets, quotes, emojis. Plain text only. No mention of "the article" or outlet names. We need 60-90 words. Let's craft ~75 words. Sentence 1: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed former Manchester United midfielder Casemiro as a free agent. Sentence 2: The 34‑year‑old Brazil international agreed to a contract running through the 2027 MLS season with an option to extend to June 2029, and Inter Miami says it acquired his discovery rights from the LA Galaxy. Sentence 3: MLS says it is gathering information and will not comment further until the review ends, at which point the settlement terms will be released. Check word count. Let's count. Sentence1: "Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed former Manchester United midfielder Casemiro as a free agent.

stories.title: Pesticidi: richiamati riso Carnaroli, riso Roma integrale e kit pasta e fagioli
stories.summary: Riso Carnaroli e Roma integrale richiamati per pesticidi oltre i limiti. Il Ministero della Salute e le catene di supermercati hanno segnalato nuovi richiami: riso Carnaroli Tenca Colombo & C. con tetrametrina e riso Roma integrale Riso Vignola con clorpirifos, vietato in UE. I prodotti sono stati venduti in confezioni da 1 kg, con lotto 13022036 e TMC 31/03/2027. Il clorpirifos, presente nel riso Roma, è un insetticida organofosfato con possibili effetti neurotossici, soprattutto sui bambini.

stories.title: Il faudra l'aval de l'employeur ou de France Travail avant de suivre une formation: le gouvernement veut (encore) réformer le CPF et cela suscite déjà des remous
stories.summary: The French government plans to reform the Compte Personnel de Formation (CPF), a program allowing workers to fund their own training, by requiring employer approval or validation from France Travail or Association for the Employment of Executives (Apec) before accessing the funds. This change would affect approximately 45 million workers, who would need to justify the relevance of their training to their professional goals and the job market. The reform is expected to be included in the 2027 budget. The French Confederation of Christian Workers (CFDT) has already expressed opposition to the plan.

stories.title: The iPhone 18 Pro's Rumored Camera Upgrade, Explained
stories.summary: Apple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max are expected to feature a variable-aperture camera, a first for the company's smartphone lineup. This upgrade would allow for smoother-looking video when shooting outdoors and improved low-light flexibility. Production for the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max reportedly began in July 2026, with a September launch expected. The new camera technology is set to be paired with Apple's first-ever foldable iPhone.

stories.title: iPhone 18 news, leaks, and rumors: Release date, iPhone 18 Pro details, more info
stories.summary: Apple is expected to unveil its next iPhone lineup in September 2026, including the iPhone 18 Pro, 18 Pro Max, and a rumored foldable iPhone Ultra. The standard iPhone 18 and new iPhone Air may arrive in early 2027. Apple’s iOS 27, featuring Siri AI, will power the devices. The company has historically announced new iPhones in September.

stories.title: Man, 30s, dies after being struck by van in Co Kilkenny
stories.summary: A man in his 30s has died after being struck by a van on the R693 near Arigan Bridge in Co Kilkenny at 9:30am on Wednesday. This is the 101st road fatality in Ireland so far this year. Gardaí are appealing for witnesses to come forward with any camera footage or information. The road has since reopened, and a post-mortem examination will be carried out at University Hospital Waterford.

stories.title: Nexsen Establishes Hong Kong Research Facility to Tap US$5.36 Billion Asia-Pacific Diagnostics Market
stories.summary: Nexsen Limited has established a research and development facility in Hong Kong to tap into the US$5.36 billion Asia-Pacific diagnostics market. The facility will support development and clinical validation of diagnostic technologies targeting traumatic brain injury and neonatal sepsis. Nexsen aims to pursue regional market entry in the Asia-Pacific region. The company's new laboratory positions it closer to Asian healthcare regulators and target populations.

stories.title: Mid-air mass brawl halts easyJet's Tenerife to Liverpool flight
stories.summary: A mid-air brawl involving around 10 passengers forced an easyJet flight from Tenerife to Liverpool to return to the Spanish island, where police were waiting. The 186-passenger aircraft departed 30 minutes before turning back due to safety concerns. The plane then continued to Liverpool after meeting with authorities. The incident highlights the growing issue of disruptive behavior on flights.

stories.title: Preliminary independent autopsy results for Nolan Wells leave key questions unanswered
stories.summary: Weeks after an 18-year-old’s body was found floating face-down near an island off the Mississippi coast, an independent autopsy requested by his family has yielded no solid answers about what led to his death. An independent pathologist described Nolan Wells’ manner of death as undetermined pending further investigation, his preliminary autopsy report says. The teen’s body was found July 6 along the shoreline of Horn Island Wells’ death sparked nationwide attention and speculation that race may have played a role, given Mississippi’s fraught racial history. Photos from the trip showed the Black teen surrounded by White friends, though at least one other Black friend was also on the trip.

stories.title: Zendaya faces backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at Odyssey event
stories.summary: American actress Zendaya is facing backlash for wearing earrings featuring ancient Iranian gold plaques from the Ziwiye hoard, a 2,700-year-old treasure discovered in northwestern Iran in 1947, at a London press event for the film Odyssey. The earrings, created in 2018, are attributed to the Ziwiye hoard and have sparked a debate over cultural heritage and the ethics of using ancient artifacts in luxury fashion. The controversy has reignited discussion over Kurdish cultural heritage and the responsibilities of owning and displaying antiquities. The incident has also raised questions about the ownership and display of the Ziwiye hoard's artifacts.

stories.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care, Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen win
stories.summary: Cameron Menzies has withdrawn from the World Matchplay after suffering from high blood pressure and fainting during his second-round match in Blackpool. The PDC confirmed the Scotsman was cleared by medical staff but unable to continue against Ross Smith. Elsewhere, Gerwyn Price and Gian van Veen secured quarter-final spots with victories over Rob Cross and Wessel Nijman. These results set the stage for the tournament's next round of high-stakes competition.

stories.title: Shownieuws creëert verwarring over lot Greetje in Urk!: 'Doet echt niet meer mee'
stories.summary: We need to write a 3-4 sentence brief, 60-90 words, for a short news video. Lead with most important fact. Include key who/what/where and number/date/consequence. Add one line of context or what happens next. Must be neutral, factual, no hype, no opinion, no fabrication. No repeated words or sentences. No markdown, no hashtags, no brackets, no quotes, no emoji. Also cannot mention "the article" or outlet names. Must cross-check the excerpts. Let's parse the content: From tvgids.nl: - There is confusion about Greetje Hakvoort's participation in Urk! (reality series). She moved to Emmeloord, leading Evert Santegoeds to insinuate that this ends her role. Both SBS6 and Greetje denied that. Shownieuws now creates new confusion. The entertainment program has Teun Kapitein (Teun Föhn) as guest in studio, and presenter Fien Vermeulen tells him that Greetje will no longer be seen. However, Teun says earlier that she is still participating ("Ze draait nu nog gewoon mee in de serie"). The zender (channel) said it's not an issue that Greetje leaves Urk! (i.e., she hasn't left). Teun finds it a weird story. Fien says "She has told us she will not continue in the series." So contradictory statements: Teun says she still participates; Fien says she will not. The zender says it's not an issue that she leaves (implying she hasn't left). There's confusion. From kijk.nl: It's just a newsletter sign-up, no relevant info about the event. So we ignore that. Thus the key fact: Greetje Hakvoort's participation in Urk! is in dispute; she moved to Emmeloord, and there is confusion whether she will continue or not. The most important fact: there is contradictory statements about her continuing in the series. We need to include key who/what/where and number/date/consequence.

stories.title: Elon Musk says Grok Imagine will make ‘historically accurate’ AI adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey
stories.summary: Elon Musk announced on July 22, 2026, that Grok Imagine will produce a full-length, "historically accurate" AI adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey by year’s end. The tech mogul criticized Christopher Nolan’s recent live-action film, questioning its casting choices. Musk also shared an AI-generated clip of Odysseus and Calypso, though Homer’s epic remains a work of fiction. The project follows Musk’s ongoing public dispute over the blockbuster’s production choices.

stories.title: Spain vs Argentina odds boost: get 50-1 on over 1.5 goals in the final with Ladbrokes
stories.summary: Spain and Argentina face off in the World Cup final with over 1.5 goals priced at 50-1. The match pits Europe’s champions against South America’s defending champions, who also hold the World Cup title. Spain scored 13 goals en route to the final, while Argentina netted 17, including a dramatic semi-final win over England. Messi aims for a final triumph in what could be his last World Cup game.

stories.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
stories.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine set has been created, featuring a working game with a joystick and button controls. The set includes an endless series of barrels for Mario to jump, using the same original barrels from Lego's 1989 pirate sets. The machine is fully functional, but does not play exactly like the classic arcade game. Shigeru Miyamoto, creator of Mario, is reportedly pleased with the set.

stories.title: One Morning of Controlled Chaos in Maine
stories.summary: A Maine Senate seat remains vacant after Graham Platner's sudden withdrawal, prompting a chaotic two-week nomination process. Over 200 delegates gathered in Sanford to vote for a replacement, with the winner to be chosen by the Democratic State Committee. The process has been marred by controversy and misinformation, with some voters and party members questioning its democratic legitimacy. The winner will be announced in the coming days.

stories.title: Tak powitano Lewandowskiego. Nagranie obiegło sieć
stories.summary: Robert Lewandowski made his debut in Major League Soccer, starting for Chicago Fire in a match against Inter Miami. The 37-year-old Polish striker played his first official game in the US league after four seasons with FC Barcelona. Lewandowski's arrival in the MLS was welcomed by the league, with an official video message on social media. He will look to make an impact in his new team as they continue their season.

stories.title: Alphabet earnings updates: Stock sinks during analyst call as company hikes 2026 capex
stories.summary: Google's stock sank during an analyst call as the company increased its 2026 capital expenditure forecast to as much as $205 billion. Alphabet's CEO Sundar Pichai emphasized scaling the business and executing on its potential, particularly with its Waymo autonomous taxi service. The company is also investing heavily in its Gemini 4 AI model, aiming to release new models almost every month. This significant spending hike may impact Alphabet's financial performance in the coming year.

stories.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
stories.summary: Camel herders in Africa's Afar region are struggling to care for their animals due to extreme heat, with 8 camel calves lost in the past month alone. Temperatures in the region have become increasingly intense, causing blisters, watery eyes, and skin burns in the camels. The Afar region in north-eastern Ethiopia is one of the hottest and driest places on Earth. As a result, herders are seeking relief measures to protect their camels.

stories.title: Elliot Page presenta la fidanzata Julia Shiplett: chi è la comica al suo fianco sul red carpet di The Odyssey
stories.summary: Elliot Page's girlfriend Julia Shiplett made a rare public appearance at The Odyssey event. The 32-year-old comedian and writer is known for her work in TV shows such as Crashing and American Dad, as well as her own comedy sketches on social media. Shiplett has also written for prestigious publications like the New York Times and has been featured in the series Overcompensating. She will likely continue to support her partner in the entertainment industry.

stories.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
stories.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark nuclear deal, a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement," allowing the Gulf kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear program. The deal includes a "bilateral safeguards agreement" and could provide great access for American companies in the Saudi nuclear energy program. The agreement marks a significant step in advancing US economic and strategic objectives, including nuclear nonproliferation. The deal's text remains undisclosed, but experts warn of potential future nuclear proliferation risks in the region.

stories.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has targeted Canada's dairy supply management system, imposing a 50% tariff on $20 billion worth of Canadian goods imported to the US, set to take effect in August. The system, which includes production quotas, set pricing, and import quotas on dairy, eggs, and poultry, is seen as "unreasonable" by American farmers. Canadian politicians have indicated they will not negotiate on the issue, with Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette calling supply management non-negotiable. The move is likely to spark a trade dispute between the two countries.

stories.title: Inter Miami vs Chicago Fire live online: score, stats, goals & updates | MLS 2026
stories.summary: Inter Miami CF and Chicago Fire FC are tied 1-1 at halftime in their MLS match. The score remains unchanged after 45 minutes of play, with both teams having equal opportunities to take the lead. The second half is set to begin with four minutes of added time. The match will continue with a high level of intensity as both teams look to gain an advantage in the second half.

stories.title: Oil extends gains as Trump threatens strikes on critical Iranian infrastructure
stories.summary: Oil prices surged 2% to $95.99 per barrel as US President Donald Trump threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure in response to any attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. This move raises concerns of further escalation in the conflict disrupting crude supplies. Trump's warning specifically targets Iranian infrastructure, including bridges and power plants. The US and Iran are now locked in a high-stakes standoff, with Iran vowing to retaliate against US-linked assets in the region.

stories.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
stories.summary: New research suggests that up to 86% of raw sewage spills in England may be illegal, with most occurring during normal weather conditions, not heavy rain. The study analyzed over 350 storm overflow outlets across nine water companies serving over 30 million people. This raises concerns about the compliance of England's water companies with environmental regulations. The findings could lead to increased scrutiny and potential enforcement action against the companies.

stories.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
stories.summary: Up to 5,000 people in England with multiple sclerosis will now have access to the life-changing drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to aid mobility. The NHS in England has made the medication available, following clinical trials showing improvements in walking speed for nearly 40% of users. This decision could significantly impact daily life for those affected, enabling them to live independently and participate in social activities. The drug is already available in other parts of the UK.

stories.title: talkSPORT tips – Best football bets and expert advice for Wednesday 22 July
stories.summary: The World Cup hiatus ends as Major League Soccer resumes Wednesday, July 22, with New England Revolution hosting Toronto FC. Toronto enters on a nine-match winless MLS run, while New England has conceded seven home goals in seven matches. Inter Miami faces Chicago Fire in a marquee match featuring Hugo Cuypers to score, over 2.5 goals and over 10.5 corners. The league’s restart marks the shift from international to club football across Europe and North America.

stories.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
stories.summary: Spain midfielder Gavi believes Argentina's Leandro Paredes should not be suspended for his role in a post-match confrontation following the World Cup final. Paredes appeared to push Spain's Eric Garcia by the throat, while Gavi was forced to the ground by Argentina's Thiago Almada. A disciplinary and ethics prosecutor has been appointed to assess the incident. The outcome could result in Paredes' suspension and a fine for the Argentine Football Association.

stories.title: Opinion | Amnesty International UK vs. J.K. Rowling
stories.summary: Amnesty International UK has published a report criticizing individuals with "gender critical" views as "anti-rights." The report, titled "A Growing Threat: The Anti-Rights Movement in the UK," was released on July 8. Critics, including author J.K. Rowling, have accused Amnesty International of undermining its core principles by taking a stance on a social issue. The controversy is likely to continue, with Amnesty International's reputation at stake.

stories.title: Vincenzo Italiano: "Çok kaliteli bir oyuncu, beni şaşırttı"
stories.summary: Beşiktaş Teknik Direktörü Vincenzo Italiano, UEFA Avrupa Ligi'nde Midtjylland ile oynayacakları karşılaşma öncesinde takımın hazır olduğunu belirterek, sahaya Beşiktaş kimliğini yansıtan bir ekip çıkarmayı hedeflediğini söyledi. Italiano, sezon öncesi kampının milli takımlarda bulunan oyuncular nedeniyle eksik kadroyla başladığını ifade etti. Beşiktaş, Midtjylland ile oynayacakları karşılaşma 15 Temmuz'da oynanacak. Italiano, takımın hazırlık sürecinde genç futbolcularla çalıştığını ve son 10 günde tam kadro çalışmaya başladıklarını söyledi.

stories.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
stories.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have risen by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives, with Gatwick aiming to reduce car usage by encouraging passengers to arrive by trains and buses. London City Airport now charges £8 for a five-minute drop-off, equivalent to £1.60 per minute. This move contrasts with EU airports, where none of the top 10 charge for drop-offs.

stories.title: Jeff Bezos holds talks with group interested in buying Liverpool stake: reports
stories.summary: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has discussed a potential minority stake purchase in Liverpool with a consortium led by Amit Bhatia. Reports on Wednesday confirmed talks between Bezos and the group, which has shown interest in investing in the Premier League club. The consortium’s proposal remains under review. Liverpool has not commented on the matter.

stories.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Amid Push for Private Security
stories.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

stories.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
stories.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit two warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries, killing seven workers and injuring dozens in the Tambov region. The attacks, which also targeted a warehouse east of Moscow, have disrupted supplies to the Russian army and frustrated hundreds of Russian sellers who depend on online marketplaces for their livelihoods. The damage is estimated at £750m. The strikes mark a new escalation in Ukraine's campaign against Russia's logistics hubs.

stories.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has received a four-match touchline ban for criticizing a late penalty decision in a May match against Motherwell. The ban is in addition to an immediate three-game suspension with one game suspended until the end of the season. McInnes and Rangers plan to appeal the decision, which they argue was directed at the specific call rather than a broader criticism of officials. The case will now proceed to an appeal hearing.

stories.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, due to soaring spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI costs are expected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion. Alphabet's combined quarterly revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but its stock fell 4% in after-hours trading. The company's growing capital expenditures, mainly for AI-related servers and data centers, are driving this financial shift.

stories.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
stories.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

stories.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
stories.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks another collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who developed the show and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. This is Yeon's latest project following his work on "Hellbound" and "Parasyte: The Grey." The series is expected to premiere next year.

stories.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
stories.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington will carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games, a huge honour for the pair. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowler, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judo gold medalist, will lead the Scottish team into the competition. The flag handover is set to take place on a specific day, although the exact date is not specified. The event marks a special moment for the athletes, who have been sworn to secrecy about the news for weeks.

stories.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
stories.summary: A 3-hour screening of Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" in 4DX format has left some viewers shaken and soaked. The 4DX experience, featuring seat-rumbling and water-spritzing technology, is available in select theaters. Over 100 people have reported experiencing seasickness and discomfort during the film. Theaters are now advising viewers to take precautions before attending a 4DX screening.

stories.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
stories.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing 23-year-old Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The Reds aim to beat Arsenal and Bayern Munich to Barcola's signature. Talks around a new deal are ongoing for Manchester United's Portugal midfielder Bruno Fernandes, 31, who has committed his future to the club. Fernandes' decision comes as Liverpool and Arsenal vie for Barcola's services.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported a quadrupled profit of $112.1 billion for the three months ending in June, driven by significant investments in artificial intelligence. Revenue reached $119.8 billion, a 24 percent increase from the previous year. The company's cloud business saw an 82 percent surge to $24.8 billion. Alphabet's major investments in A.I.-related companies like SpaceX and Anthropic contributed $77 billion to overall profits.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz persist. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran, with Tehran warning against targeting its nuclear facilities. The potential targets include structures in the capital Tehran and near the city of Natanz. The US has already launched strikes on Iran, prompting a warning of reprisals from Tehran.

stories.title: ‘Doctor Strange’ Director Says Elon Musk ‘Doesn’t Know S— About Cinema’ After Musk Vows to Make AI ‘Odyssey’: ‘His Opinions on Art Are Worthless’
stories.summary: Elon Musk has vowed to create an AI-generated film adaptation of Homer's "The Odyssey" by the end of the year. Director Scott Derrickson has publicly criticized Musk's plan, stating that he "doesn't know shit about cinema" and that his opinions on art are worthless. Musk has been critical of Christopher Nolan's adaptation of "The Odyssey" due to its casting decisions. Derrickson's comments come as Musk continues to promote his AI tool Grok, which he claims will produce a historically accurate film.

stories.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
stories.summary: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report targets ultra-processed foods, specifically those high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee recommends a new framework to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable, shifting focus from fat, salt, and sugar to sugar and refined carbohydrates. This change aims to promote healthier food options and reduce obesity in Britain.

offbeat.title: Lotto 6aus49 am Mittwoch: Das sind die Gewinnzahlen vom 22.7.2026
offbeat.summary: A 50-million-euro jackpot in the German Lotto 6aus49 was up for grabs on July 22, 2026, with a 1 in 140 million chance of winning. The winning numbers were 3, 5, 10, 14, 25, and 49, with a superzahl of 3. The jackpot remains unclaimed, and players can still check their tickets for a possible win.

offbeat.title: ‘Terrifying’: Woman says landlord was hiding behind wall of Wexford holiday home
offbeat.summary: A woman and her family were left "terrified" after discovering a landlord hiding behind a wall in a Co Wexford holiday home they had booked through Booking.com. The incident occurred last weekend when Therese and her family returned from a family party to find the landlord behind the wall. Booking.com has launched an investigation and suspended the property while the investigation is completed. The family's booking was for the entire property, which was advertised as owner-free.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay Darts: Updates, video, analysis, scores, commentary with Gerwyn Price, James Wade, Gian van Veen in action
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies has been eliminated from the 2026 World Matchplay Darts after a stunning upset on Monday night, knocking out Luke Humphries with a memorable 170 checkout. Menzies now faces Ross Smith, who has won two titles this season and opened the tournament with a 10-6 victory over Kevin Doets. Smith averaged 100.55 and fired in seven 180s in his first match. The two players will face off in the next round.

offbeat.title: « Je suis de retour » : pour la quatrième fois, Kylian Mbappé va figurer sur la pochette d'EA Sports FC
offbeat.summary: Kylian Mbappé returns as the cover athlete for EA Sports FC 27, marking his fourth appearance on the franchise's cover, equaling Lionel Messi's record. The Real Madrid striker, who last featured on the cover four years ago while at Paris Saint-Germain, will appear on the game set for release on September 25, 2026. His selection follows his standout performance as the all-time top scorer in World Cup history. The announcement comes after EA Sports revealed Mbappé will headline the Ultimate Edition, succeeding Jude Bellingham and Jamal Musiala on the standard cover.

offbeat.title: Le PSG a fait une grosse offre à Ferran Torres
offbeat.summary: Paris Saint-Germain has made a lucrative offer to Spanish striker Ferran Torres following his decisive winning goal in the 2026 World Cup final. The twenty-six-year-old forward is currently at odds with FC Barcelona over contract terms and playing time under new manager Hansi Flick. While the Catalan club intends to retain him, Torres has reportedly rejected their latest proposal. This summer transfer window could see the international star move to Paris.

offbeat.title: IFC: First Look at The Stunt Driver
offbeat.summary: We need to write a punchy 3-4 sentence brief, 60-90 words, for a short news video. Lead with most important fact. Include key who/what/where and number, date or consequence that matters. Add one line of context or what happens next. Prefer facts that agree across outlets; ignore contradictions. No repeated words or sentences. No markdown, hashtags, brackets, quotes, emojis. No mention of "the article" or outlet names. Plain text only. We need to extract agreed facts: - Film: The Stunt Driver - Starring: Jay Baruchel as Ken Carter (Canadian daredevil) - Also starring: Laurence Leboeuf, Dan Bakkedahl, Ben Foster, Ed Helms - Written and directed by Michael Dowse - Set in mid-seventies, true underdog story of Ken Carter, world champion stunt driver, attempts mile-long jump across St. Lawrence river in a rocket car. - Release: U.S. theaters on September 25 (year? The blu-ray.com says 2026 film set to open September 25. Deadline says Independent Film Company releases the film in U.S. theaters on September 25. No year given but likely 2026? The agreed fact: release date September 25 (year unspecified but we can include 2026 if agreed? blu-ray.com says 2026, deadline doesn't mention year but says release September 25.

offbeat.title: Las Feministas del PSOE y 96 asociaciones de España y Latinoamérica cargan contra Bibiana Aído ante ONU por atacar a la relatora de Mujer
offbeat.summary: The user wants a 3-4 sentence brief (60-90 words) for a short news video based on three Spanish news sources reporting the same event. I need to cross-check facts that agree across outlets. Key facts from all three sources: - Bibiana Aído (former Spanish Equality Minister under Zapatero, now UN Women Regional Director for Latin America/Caribbean based in Panama) - 96+ feminist associations (including FEMAS, Spanish Socialist Feminists Association led by Amelia Valcárcel) from Spain and Latin America (Brazil, Mexico, Colombia) - They sent a letter to UN Secretary General António Guterres - Accusing Aído of attacking/difaming Reem Alsalem (UN Special Rapporteur on Violence Against Women) - Aído distributed a confidential report (from UN Women Brazil office) on June 10 to discredit Alsalem - The report criticized Alsalem's visits to Mexico, Brazil, Colombia - Associations accuse Aído of promoting "queer/trans" ideology, normalizing prostitution as "sex work" and surrogacy as a right All three agree on the core: feminist groups complain to Guterres about Aído targeting Alsalem. Need to lead with most important fact: 96+ feminist associations filed complaint with UN chief against Aído for attacking the UN women's rights rapporteur. Word count target: 60-90 words. 3-4 sentences. Draft: More than 90 feminist organizations from Spain and Latin America have formally complained to UN Secretary-General António Guterres against Bibiana Aído, the UN Women regional director for Latin America.

offbeat.title: Princess Charlene Glitters in a Gold Elie Saab Gown at Monaco’s Red Cross Ball
offbeat.summary: We need to produce a 3-4 sentence brief, 60-90 words, punchy, informative, lead with most important fact, include key who/what/where and number, date or consequence that matters; add one line of context or what happens next. Prefer facts that agree across outlets; ignore contradictions. Neutral, factual, no hype, no opinion, no fabrication, no repeated words or sentences. Plain text only — no markdown, hashtags, brackets, quotes, emoji. Do not mention "the article" or outlet names. We have two sources: townandcountrymag.com and hola.com. They both mention Princess Charlene and Prince Albert headlined Monaco’s Red Cross Ball on Saturday, one of the marquee events on the principality’s summer social calendar. They both mention the event date? townandcountrymag.com says "Saturday" but not specific date; hola.com says "July 18" (the 77th Monaco Red Cross Gala). So the date is July 18 (Saturday). Both mention the location: Salle des Étoiles in Monte Carlo (townandcountrymag.com) and the gala (hola.com). They both mention Princess Charlene wearing Elie Saab gown; hola.com mentions soft metallic and ivory tones, coordinated look. They both mention Camille Gottlieb, niece, wearing Elisabetta Franchi gown (townandcountrymag.com) and also mentions her previous homage to Grace Kelly. So we can include that Camille Gottlieb attended, wore a soft yellow Elisabetta Franchi gown. Both mention Prince Albert and Princess Charlene as president and vice president of Monaco Red Cross.

offbeat.title: Obama Takes Shot at Vance Over Marriage ‘Hypocrisy’
offbeat.summary: Former President Barack Obama criticized Vice President JD Vance for his stance on immigration, pointing out the hypocrisy of Vance's hardline rhetoric given his own marriage to an immigrant. Vance's wife, Usha, received US citizenship at birth after her parents immigrated from India. Obama suggested Vance's views echo "blood-and-soil" ideas about citizenship. The criticism comes as Vance promotes MAGA ideas on immigration.

offbeat.title: Casemiro: Inter Miami being investigated for tampering after signing ex-Manchester United midfielder
offbeat.summary: We need to produce a punchy, informative 3-4 sentence brief (60-90 words). Lead with most important fact: MLS investigating Inter Miami for tampering after signing Casemiro. Include who/what/where and number/date/consequence that matters: Casemiro signed, 34-year-old, contract to end of 2027 MLS season with option to 2029, investigation about discovery rights, LA Galaxy held rights, settlement reached. Add one line of context or what happens next: MLS gathering info, will refrain comment until review complete; terms to be released after investigation. We must avoid repeated words or sentences. No markdown, hashtags, brackets, quotes, emojis. Plain text only. No mention of "the article" or outlet names. We need 60-90 words. Let's craft ~75 words. Sentence 1: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed former Manchester United midfielder Casemiro as a free agent. Sentence 2: The 34‑year‑old Brazil international agreed to a contract running through the 2027 MLS season with an option to extend to June 2029, and Inter Miami says it acquired his discovery rights from the LA Galaxy. Sentence 3: MLS says it is gathering information and will not comment further until the review ends, at which point the settlement terms will be released. Check word count. Let's count. Sentence1: "Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed former Manchester United midfielder Casemiro as a free agent.

offbeat.title: Pesticidi: richiamati riso Carnaroli, riso Roma integrale e kit pasta e fagioli
offbeat.summary: Riso Carnaroli e Roma integrale richiamati per pesticidi oltre i limiti. Il Ministero della Salute e le catene di supermercati hanno segnalato nuovi richiami: riso Carnaroli Tenca Colombo & C. con tetrametrina e riso Roma integrale Riso Vignola con clorpirifos, vietato in UE. I prodotti sono stati venduti in confezioni da 1 kg, con lotto 13022036 e TMC 31/03/2027. Il clorpirifos, presente nel riso Roma, è un insetticida organofosfato con possibili effetti neurotossici, soprattutto sui bambini.

offbeat.title: Il faudra l'aval de l'employeur ou de France Travail avant de suivre une formation: le gouvernement veut (encore) réformer le CPF et cela suscite déjà des remous
offbeat.summary: The French government plans to reform the Compte Personnel de Formation (CPF), a program allowing workers to fund their own training, by requiring employer approval or validation from France Travail or Association for the Employment of Executives (Apec) before accessing the funds. This change would affect approximately 45 million workers, who would need to justify the relevance of their training to their professional goals and the job market. The reform is expected to be included in the 2027 budget. The French Confederation of Christian Workers (CFDT) has already expressed opposition to the plan.

offbeat.title: The iPhone 18 Pro's Rumored Camera Upgrade, Explained
offbeat.summary: Apple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max are expected to feature a variable-aperture camera, a first for the company's smartphone lineup. This upgrade would allow for smoother-looking video when shooting outdoors and improved low-light flexibility. Production for the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max reportedly began in July 2026, with a September launch expected. The new camera technology is set to be paired with Apple's first-ever foldable iPhone.

offbeat.title: iPhone 18 news, leaks, and rumors: Release date, iPhone 18 Pro details, more info
offbeat.summary: Apple is expected to unveil its next iPhone lineup in September 2026, including the iPhone 18 Pro, 18 Pro Max, and a rumored foldable iPhone Ultra. The standard iPhone 18 and new iPhone Air may arrive in early 2027. Apple’s iOS 27, featuring Siri AI, will power the devices. The company has historically announced new iPhones in September.

offbeat.title: Man, 30s, dies after being struck by van in Co Kilkenny
offbeat.summary: A man in his 30s has died after being struck by a van on the R693 near Arigan Bridge in Co Kilkenny at 9:30am on Wednesday. This is the 101st road fatality in Ireland so far this year. Gardaí are appealing for witnesses to come forward with any camera footage or information. The road has since reopened, and a post-mortem examination will be carried out at University Hospital Waterford.

offbeat.title: Nexsen Establishes Hong Kong Research Facility to Tap US$5.36 Billion Asia-Pacific Diagnostics Market
offbeat.summary: Nexsen Limited has established a research and development facility in Hong Kong to tap into the US$5.36 billion Asia-Pacific diagnostics market. The facility will support development and clinical validation of diagnostic technologies targeting traumatic brain injury and neonatal sepsis. Nexsen aims to pursue regional market entry in the Asia-Pacific region. The company's new laboratory positions it closer to Asian healthcare regulators and target populations.

offbeat.title: Mid-air mass brawl halts easyJet's Tenerife to Liverpool flight
offbeat.summary: A mid-air brawl involving around 10 passengers forced an easyJet flight from Tenerife to Liverpool to return to the Spanish island, where police were waiting. The 186-passenger aircraft departed 30 minutes before turning back due to safety concerns. The plane then continued to Liverpool after meeting with authorities. The incident highlights the growing issue of disruptive behavior on flights.

offbeat.title: Preliminary independent autopsy results for Nolan Wells leave key questions unanswered
offbeat.summary: Weeks after an 18-year-old’s body was found floating face-down near an island off the Mississippi coast, an independent autopsy requested by his family has yielded no solid answers about what led to his death. An independent pathologist described Nolan Wells’ manner of death as undetermined pending further investigation, his preliminary autopsy report says. The teen’s body was found July 6 along the shoreline of Horn Island Wells’ death sparked nationwide attention and speculation that race may have played a role, given Mississippi’s fraught racial history. Photos from the trip showed the Black teen surrounded by White friends, though at least one other Black friend was also on the trip.

offbeat.title: Zendaya faces backlash for wearing ancient Iranian jewellery at Odyssey event
offbeat.summary: American actress Zendaya is facing backlash for wearing earrings featuring ancient Iranian gold plaques from the Ziwiye hoard, a 2,700-year-old treasure discovered in northwestern Iran in 1947, at a London press event for the film Odyssey. The earrings, created in 2018, are attributed to the Ziwiye hoard and have sparked a debate over cultural heritage and the ethics of using ancient artifacts in luxury fashion. The controversy has reignited discussion over Kurdish cultural heritage and the responsibilities of owning and displaying antiquities. The incident has also raised questions about the ownership and display of the Ziwiye hoard's artifacts.

offbeat.title: World Matchplay 2026: Cameron Menzies receives medical care, Gerwyn Price, James Wade and Gian van Veen win
offbeat.summary: Cameron Menzies has withdrawn from the World Matchplay after suffering from high blood pressure and fainting during his second-round match in Blackpool. The PDC confirmed the Scotsman was cleared by medical staff but unable to continue against Ross Smith. Elsewhere, Gerwyn Price and Gian van Veen secured quarter-final spots with victories over Rob Cross and Wessel Nijman. These results set the stage for the tournament's next round of high-stakes competition.

offbeat.title: Shownieuws creëert verwarring over lot Greetje in Urk!: 'Doet echt niet meer mee'
offbeat.summary: We need to write a 3-4 sentence brief, 60-90 words, for a short news video. Lead with most important fact. Include key who/what/where and number/date/consequence. Add one line of context or what happens next. Must be neutral, factual, no hype, no opinion, no fabrication. No repeated words or sentences. No markdown, no hashtags, no brackets, no quotes, no emoji. Also cannot mention "the article" or outlet names. Must cross-check the excerpts. Let's parse the content: From tvgids.nl: - There is confusion about Greetje Hakvoort's participation in Urk! (reality series). She moved to Emmeloord, leading Evert Santegoeds to insinuate that this ends her role. Both SBS6 and Greetje denied that. Shownieuws now creates new confusion. The entertainment program has Teun Kapitein (Teun Föhn) as guest in studio, and presenter Fien Vermeulen tells him that Greetje will no longer be seen. However, Teun says earlier that she is still participating ("Ze draait nu nog gewoon mee in de serie"). The zender (channel) said it's not an issue that Greetje leaves Urk! (i.e., she hasn't left). Teun finds it a weird story. Fien says "She has told us she will not continue in the series." So contradictory statements: Teun says she still participates; Fien says she will not. The zender says it's not an issue that she leaves (implying she hasn't left). There's confusion. From kijk.nl: It's just a newsletter sign-up, no relevant info about the event. So we ignore that. Thus the key fact: Greetje Hakvoort's participation in Urk! is in dispute; she moved to Emmeloord, and there is confusion whether she will continue or not. The most important fact: there is contradictory statements about her continuing in the series. We need to include key who/what/where and number/date/consequence.

offbeat.title: Elon Musk says Grok Imagine will make ‘historically accurate’ AI adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey
offbeat.summary: Elon Musk announced on July 22, 2026, that Grok Imagine will produce a full-length, "historically accurate" AI adaptation of Homer’s Odyssey by year’s end. The tech mogul criticized Christopher Nolan’s recent live-action film, questioning its casting choices. Musk also shared an AI-generated clip of Odysseus and Calypso, though Homer’s epic remains a work of fiction. The project follows Musk’s ongoing public dispute over the blockbuster’s production choices.

offbeat.title: Spain vs Argentina odds boost: get 50-1 on over 1.5 goals in the final with Ladbrokes
offbeat.summary: Spain and Argentina face off in the World Cup final with over 1.5 goals priced at 50-1. The match pits Europe’s champions against South America’s defending champions, who also hold the World Cup title. Spain scored 13 goals en route to the final, while Argentina netted 17, including a dramatic semi-final win over England. Messi aims for a final triumph in what could be his last World Cup game.

offbeat.title: One Morning of Controlled Chaos in Maine
offbeat.summary: A Maine Senate seat remains vacant after Graham Platner's sudden withdrawal, prompting a chaotic two-week nomination process. Over 200 delegates gathered in Sanford to vote for a replacement, with the winner to be chosen by the Democratic State Committee. The process has been marred by controversy and misinformation, with some voters and party members questioning its democratic legitimacy. The winner will be announced in the coming days.

offbeat.title: Tak powitano Lewandowskiego. Nagranie obiegło sieć
offbeat.summary: Robert Lewandowski made his debut in Major League Soccer, starting for Chicago Fire in a match against Inter Miami. The 37-year-old Polish striker played his first official game in the US league after four seasons with FC Barcelona. Lewandowski's arrival in the MLS was welcomed by the league, with an official video message on social media. He will look to make an impact in his new team as they continue their season.

offbeat.title: Alphabet earnings updates: Stock sinks during analyst call as company hikes 2026 capex
offbeat.summary: Google's stock sank during an analyst call as the company increased its 2026 capital expenditure forecast to as much as $205 billion. Alphabet's CEO Sundar Pichai emphasized scaling the business and executing on its potential, particularly with its Waymo autonomous taxi service. The company is also investing heavily in its Gemini 4 AI model, aiming to release new models almost every month. This significant spending hike may impact Alphabet's financial performance in the coming year.

offbeat.title: Even camels can't cope: Africa's ships of the desert hit by rising temperatures
offbeat.summary: Camel herders in Africa's Afar region are struggling to care for their animals due to extreme heat, with 8 camel calves lost in the past month alone. Temperatures in the region have become increasingly intense, causing blisters, watery eyes, and skin burns in the camels. The Afar region in north-eastern Ethiopia is one of the hottest and driest places on Earth. As a result, herders are seeking relief measures to protect their camels.

offbeat.title: Elliot Page presenta la fidanzata Julia Shiplett: chi è la comica al suo fianco sul red carpet di The Odyssey
offbeat.summary: Elliot Page's girlfriend Julia Shiplett made a rare public appearance at The Odyssey event. The 32-year-old comedian and writer is known for her work in TV shows such as Crashing and American Dad, as well as her own comedy sketches on social media. Shiplett has also written for prestigious publications like the New York Times and has been featured in the series Overcompensating. She will likely continue to support her partner in the entertainment industry.

offbeat.title: Inter Miami vs Chicago Fire live online: score, stats, goals & updates | MLS 2026
offbeat.summary: Inter Miami CF and Chicago Fire FC are tied 1-1 at halftime in their MLS match. The score remains unchanged after 45 minutes of play, with both teams having equal opportunities to take the lead. The second half is set to begin with four minutes of added time. The match will continue with a high level of intensity as both teams look to gain an advantage in the second half.

offbeat.title: talkSPORT tips – Best football bets and expert advice for Wednesday 22 July
offbeat.summary: The World Cup hiatus ends as Major League Soccer resumes Wednesday, July 22, with New England Revolution hosting Toronto FC. Toronto enters on a nine-match winless MLS run, while New England has conceded seven home goals in seven matches. Inter Miami faces Chicago Fire in a marquee match featuring Hugo Cuypers to score, over 2.5 goals and over 10.5 corners. The league’s restart marks the shift from international to club football across Europe and North America.

offbeat.title: World Cup 2026: Gavi does not think Paredes should be suspended
offbeat.summary: Spain midfielder Gavi believes Argentina's Leandro Paredes should not be suspended for his role in a post-match confrontation following the World Cup final. Paredes appeared to push Spain's Eric Garcia by the throat, while Gavi was forced to the ground by Argentina's Thiago Almada. A disciplinary and ethics prosecutor has been appointed to assess the incident. The outcome could result in Paredes' suspension and a fine for the Argentine Football Association.

offbeat.title: Opinion | Amnesty International UK vs. J.K. Rowling
offbeat.summary: Amnesty International UK has published a report criticizing individuals with "gender critical" views as "anti-rights." The report, titled "A Growing Threat: The Anti-Rights Movement in the UK," was released on July 8. Critics, including author J.K. Rowling, have accused Amnesty International of undermining its core principles by taking a stance on a social issue. The controversy is likely to continue, with Amnesty International's reputation at stake.

offbeat.title: Vincenzo Italiano: "Çok kaliteli bir oyuncu, beni şaşırttı"
offbeat.summary: Beşiktaş Teknik Direktörü Vincenzo Italiano, UEFA Avrupa Ligi'nde Midtjylland ile oynayacakları karşılaşma öncesinde takımın hazır olduğunu belirterek, sahaya Beşiktaş kimliğini yansıtan bir ekip çıkarmayı hedeflediğini söyledi. Italiano, sezon öncesi kampının milli takımlarda bulunan oyuncular nedeniyle eksik kadroyla başladığını ifade etti. Beşiktaş, Midtjylland ile oynayacakları karşılaşma 15 Temmuz'da oynanacak. Italiano, takımın hazırlık sürecinde genç futbolcularla çalıştığını ve son 10 günde tam kadro çalışmaya başladıklarını söyledi.

offbeat.title: Jeff Bezos holds talks with group interested in buying Liverpool stake: reports
offbeat.summary: Amazon founder Jeff Bezos has discussed a potential minority stake purchase in Liverpool with a consortium led by Amit Bhatia. Reports on Wednesday confirmed talks between Bezos and the group, which has shown interest in investing in the Premier League club. The consortium’s proposal remains under review. Liverpool has not commented on the matter.

offbeat.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
offbeat.summary: Ukrainian drones have hit two warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries, killing seven workers and injuring dozens in the Tambov region. The attacks, which also targeted a warehouse east of Moscow, have disrupted supplies to the Russian army and frustrated hundreds of Russian sellers who depend on online marketplaces for their livelihoods. The damage is estimated at £750m. The strikes mark a new escalation in Ukraine's campaign against Russia's logistics hubs.

offbeat.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
offbeat.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz persist. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran, with Tehran warning against targeting its nuclear facilities. The potential targets include structures in the capital Tehran and near the city of Natanz. The US has already launched strikes on Iran, prompting a warning of reprisals from Tehran.

tech.title: Lego’s Donkey Kong arcade machine lets Mario jump endless barrels — Miyamoto is reportedly happy
tech.summary: A Lego Donkey Kong arcade machine set has been created, featuring a working game with a joystick and button controls. The set includes an endless series of barrels for Mario to jump, using the same original barrels from Lego's 1989 pirate sets. The machine is fully functional, but does not play exactly like the classic arcade game. Shigeru Miyamoto, creator of Mario, is reportedly pleased with the set.

tech.title: Google burning through cash with spiralling AI costs
tech.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported negative free cash flow of $5.9 billion for the first time in at least a decade, due to soaring spending on artificial intelligence infrastructure. The company's AI costs are expected to reach $205 billion this year, up from $190 billion. Alphabet's combined quarterly revenue rose 23% to $119.8 billion, but its stock fell 4% in after-hours trading. The company's growing capital expenditures, mainly for AI-related servers and data centers, are driving this financial shift.

tech.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to $112 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
tech.summary: Google's parent company Alphabet reported a quadrupled profit of $112.1 billion for the three months ending in June, driven by significant investments in artificial intelligence. Revenue reached $119.8 billion, a 24 percent increase from the previous year. The company's cloud business saw an 82 percent surge to $24.8 billion. Alphabet's major investments in A.I.-related companies like SpaceX and Anthropic contributed $77 billion to overall profits.

breaking.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
breaking.summary: The US and Saudi Arabia have signed a landmark nuclear deal, a "peaceful nuclear co-operation agreement," allowing the Gulf kingdom to develop a civilian nuclear program. The deal includes a "bilateral safeguards agreement" and could provide great access for American companies in the Saudi nuclear energy program. The agreement marks a significant step in advancing US economic and strategic objectives, including nuclear nonproliferation. The deal's text remains undisclosed, but experts warn of potential future nuclear proliferation risks in the region.

breaking.title: Canada's 'powerful' dairy sector is in Trump's trade crosshairs
breaking.summary: US President Donald Trump has targeted Canada's dairy supply management system, imposing a 50% tariff on $20 billion worth of Canadian goods imported to the US, set to take effect in August. The system, which includes production quotas, set pricing, and import quotas on dairy, eggs, and poultry, is seen as "unreasonable" by American farmers. Canadian politicians have indicated they will not negotiate on the issue, with Quebec Premier Christine Fréchette calling supply management non-negotiable. The move is likely to spark a trade dispute between the two countries.

breaking.title: Three Airports Plan to Ditch T.S.A. Amid Push for Private Security
breaking.summary: The airports have opted in to the agency’s new security model, an agency spokesperson confirmed on Wednesday. A union official said the move could jeopardize public safety.

crisis.title: Oil extends gains as Trump threatens strikes on critical Iranian infrastructure
crisis.summary: Oil prices surged 2% to $95.99 per barrel as US President Donald Trump threatened to bomb Iranian infrastructure in response to any attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz. This move raises concerns of further escalation in the conflict disrupting crude supplies. Trump's warning specifically targets Iranian infrastructure, including bridges and power plants. The US and Iran are now locked in a high-stakes standoff, with Iran vowing to retaliate against US-linked assets in the region.

crisis.title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
crisis.summary: Drop-off fees at the UK's major airports have risen by a third on average since last summer, with two London airports, Gatwick and Stansted, charging the highest fees at £10. The fees are part of airports' business models and climate change objectives, with Gatwick aiming to reduce car usage by encouraging passengers to arrive by trains and buses. London City Airport now charges £8 for a five-minute drop-off, equivalent to £1.60 per minute. This move contrasts with EU airports, where none of the top 10 charge for drop-offs.

facts.title: Most sewage spills in England may be illegal, new research suggests
facts.summary: New research suggests that up to 86% of raw sewage spills in England may be illegal, with most occurring during normal weather conditions, not heavy rain. The study analyzed over 350 storm overflow outlets across nine water companies serving over 30 million people. This raises concerns about the compliance of England's water companies with environmental regulations. The findings could lead to increased scrutiny and potential enforcement action against the companies.

facts.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating in Homer’s ‘Odyssey’?
facts.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

health.title: Hope for thousands of MS patients as 'life-changing' drug now on NHS in England
health.summary: Up to 5,000 people in England with multiple sclerosis will now have access to the life-changing drug fampridine, which boosts nerve signals to aid mobility. The NHS in England has made the medication available, following clinical trials showing improvements in walking speed for nearly 40% of users. This decision could significantly impact daily life for those affected, enabling them to live independently and participate in social activities. The drug is already available in other parts of the UK.

health.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
health.summary: A British health select committee report has found that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a failure of willpower. The report targets ultra-processed foods, specifically those high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee recommends a new framework to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable, shifting focus from fat, salt, and sugar to sugar and refined carbohydrates. This change aims to promote healthier food options and reduce obesity in Britain.

sports.title: McInnes glad of Rangers backing in appeal against ban
sports.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has received a four-match touchline ban for criticizing a late penalty decision in a May match against Motherwell. The ban is in addition to an immediate three-game suspension with one game suspended until the end of the season. McInnes and Rangers plan to appeal the decision, which they argue was directed at the specific call rather than a broader criticism of officials. The case will now proceed to an appeal hearing.

sports.title: Tears and sleepless nights as Marshall and Adlington get Scotland flag honour
sports.summary: Scottish athletes Marshall and Adlington will carry the Scotland flag at the Commonwealth Games, a huge honour for the pair. Marshall, a 59-year-old bowler, and Adlington, a 39-year-old judo gold medalist, will lead the Scottish team into the competition. The flag handover is set to take place on a specific day, although the exact date is not specified. The event marks a special moment for the athletes, who have been sworn to secrecy about the news for weeks.

sports.title: Liverpool confident of signing Barcola - Thursday's gossip
sports.summary: Liverpool is confident of signing 23-year-old Paris St-Germain forward Bradley Barcola, who impressed with four goal contributions at the World Cup with France this summer. The Reds aim to beat Arsenal and Bayern Munich to Barcola's signature. Talks around a new deal are ongoing for Manchester United's Portugal midfielder Bruno Fernandes, 31, who has committed his future to the club. Fernandes' decision comes as Liverpool and Arsenal vie for Barcola's services.

politics.title: Clarity Act Mired In Debate Over Whether to Bar President From Selling Crypto
politics.summary: Democrats and Republicans are haggling about the Clarity Act, a major bill pushed by the crypto industry, as it moves closer to a Senate vote.

politics.title: With Saudi Deal, Trump Loosens Nuclear Standards for One Close Ally
politics.summary: Nuclear experts say it throws fuel on the smoldering fire of atomic proliferation, creating a destabilizing situation that could raise the risk of nuclear war.

entertainment.title: Yeon Sang-ho Returns to ‘Parasyte’ Universe With ‘Tamiya,’ Starring Shioli Kutsuna, at Netflix
entertainment.summary: South Korean filmmaker Yeon Sang-ho is returning to the "Parasyte" universe with a new live-action series, "Parasyte: Tamiya," starring Shioli Kutsuna, set to stream on Netflix in 2027. The series marks another collaboration between Netflix and Yeon, who developed the show and co-wrote the script with Ryu Yong-jae. This is Yeon's latest project following his work on "Hellbound" and "Parasyte: The Grey." The series is expected to premiere next year.

entertainment.title: Shaken, Soaked and Seasick: What It’s Like to Survive ‘The Odyssey’ in 4DX
entertainment.summary: A 3-hour screening of Christopher Nolan's "The Odyssey" in 4DX format has left some viewers shaken and soaked. The 4DX experience, featuring seat-rumbling and water-spritzing technology, is available in select theaters. Over 100 people have reported experiencing seasickness and discomfort during the film. Theaters are now advising viewers to take precautions before attending a 4DX screening.

entertainment.title: ‘Doctor Strange’ Director Says Elon Musk ‘Doesn’t Know S— About Cinema’ After Musk Vows to Make AI ‘Odyssey’: ‘His Opinions on Art Are Worthless’
entertainment.summary: Elon Musk has vowed to create an AI-generated film adaptation of Homer's "The Odyssey" by the end of the year. Director Scott Derrickson has publicly criticized Musk's plan, stating that he "doesn't know shit about cinema" and that his opinions on art are worthless. Musk has been critical of Christopher Nolan's adaptation of "The Odyssey" due to its casting decisions. Derrickson's comments come as Musk continues to promote his AI tool Grok, which he claims will produce a historically accurate film.
